standards_and_methodology:
The allocation procedure described by the European standard EN 15804+A2 has been followed.

Cut-off criteria described in EN15804+A2 has been followed: 5% of energy and materials by module and 1% of total energy and raw materials.

Following EN15804+A2, flows of biogenic carbon have been allocated according to physical properties, because the net sum of biogenic CO2 uptake and release over modules A1-C4 should be zero. Relevant data records have been adjusted accordingly.

Carbon offsetting is not allowed in the EN 15804+A2 and hence not taken into account in the calculations.
lca_software_and_database:
For the calculation of the LCA results, the software program SimaPro 9.5.1 has been used.

Databased used for modelling include:
- Ecoinvent 3.9
- Industry 2.0
system_boundaries:
System boundaries (parsed from PDF diagram — no AI)

By life-cycle stage:

Construction installation stage: A4, A5
End of life stage: C1, C2, C3, C4
Beyond the system boundaries: D

A2: Transport
A3: Manufacturing

A5: Construction

B3: Repa

C3: Waste processing
C4: Disposal
D: Recovery
production_sites: The data used for the LCA is specific for this product which is manufactured by a single manufacturer in a single production site.
cut_off_criteria:
The following processes are considered below cut-off: 
• Recycling of raw materials packaging.
• All activities and energy use related to the offices and personnel.
• Steel cassettes as packaging of one of the raw materials, as these are re-used countless times.
• Infrastructure & impacts related to the use of the machines at Aliplast's site.

Cut-off criteria described in EN15804+A2 has been followed: 5% of energy and materials by module and 1% of total energy and raw materials.
allocation:
Allocation of inputs and outputs has been necessary in the manufacturing stage A3. Allocation has been performed using physical relationship – annual usage vs. annual volume of the specific window system produced.
The allocation procedure described by the European standard EN 15804+A2 has been followed.
data_quality:
Foreground processes include:
- Raw Materials used, their packaging, transportation mode and related quantities,
- Energy mix and amount used for production,
- Types of product packaging and related quantities,
- Environmental impacts of raw materials for which EPDs were available,
- All other quantitative information. 

Generic datasets were used for:
- Extraction and manufacturing of raw materials, packaging,
- Transportation and treatment of waste from raw material packaging and production,
- Waste treatment processes,
- Transport to end-of-life treatment,
- End-of-life treatment processes and scenarios.

– Declared value is representative of the representative product – Tilled Window System, which is also a worst case (highest impacts) of the product group covered by this EPD.
time_representativeness: Manufacturer specific data have been collected for the year 2022.
geographical_representativeness:
Aliplast purchases renewable energy mix from their supplier. Renewable energy mix was modelled based on the statistics for Belgium.
GWP of this mix is 0.049 kg CO2 eq. / kWh.
impact_assessment: The characterization factors from EC-JRC were applied: EF 3.1. No additional or deviating characterisation factors were used.
additional_paragraphs:
Following processes were excluded for the inventory:
- Flows related to human activities such as employee transport and administration activity.

Only product packaging contains biogenic carbon. Following EN15804+A2, flows of biogenic carbon have been allocated according to physical properties, because the net sum of biogenic CO2 uptake and release over modules A1-C4 should be zero. Relevant data records have been adjusted accordingly.

Biogenic carbon content (kg C / FU )
Biogenic carbon content in product (at the gate) 0
Biogenic carbon content in accompanying packaging (at the gate) 1.71E-01

This EPD covers 3 types of window systems. The differences between these systems are minor - quantities of only some components vary (handles, fittings, one plastic part). Consequently, this EPD is representative of the worst-case scenario.

subsections.title: Date of LCA-study
subsections.content: The LCA study has been performed in October 2024.

subsections.title: Software
subsections.content: For the calculation of the LCA results, the software program SimaPro 9.5.1 has been used.

subsections.title: Information on allocation
subsections.content:
Allocation of inputs and outputs has been necessary in the manufacturing stage A3. Allocation has been performed using physical relationship – annual usage vs. annual volume of the specific window system produced.
The allocation procedure described by the European standard EN 15804+A2 has been followed.

subsections.title: Information on cut off
subsections.content:
The following processes are considered below cut-off: 
• Recycling of raw materials packaging.
• All activities and energy use related to the offices and personnel.
• Steel cassettes as packaging of one of the raw materials, as these are re-used countless times.
• Infrastructure & impacts related to the use of the machines at Aliplast's site.

Cut-off criteria described in EN15804+A2 has been followed: 5% of energy and materials by module and 1% of total energy and raw materials.

subsections.title: Information on excluded processes
subsections.content:
Following processes were excluded for the inventory:
- Flows related to human activities such as employee transport and administration activity.

subsections.title: Information on biogenic carbon modelling
subsections.content:
Only product packaging contains biogenic carbon. Following EN15804+A2, flows of biogenic carbon have been allocated according to physical properties, because the net sum of biogenic CO2 uptake and release over modules A1-C4 should be zero. Relevant data records have been adjusted accordingly.

Biogenic carbon content (kg C / FU )
Biogenic carbon content in product (at the gate) 0
Biogenic carbon content in accompanying packaging (at the gate) 1.71E-01

subsections.title: Information on carbon off-setting
subsections.content: Carbon offsetting is not allowed in the EN 15804+A2 and hence not taken into account in the calculations.

subsections.title: Additional or deviating characterization factors
subsections.content: The characterization factors from EC-JRC were applied: EF 3.1. No additional or deviating characterisation factors were used.

subsections.title: Description of the variability
subsections.content: This EPD covers 3 types of window systems. The differences between these systems are minor - quantities of only some components vary (handles, fittings, one plastic part). Consequently, this EPD is representative of the worst-case scenario.

subsections.title: Specificity
subsections.content: The data used for the LCA is specific for this product which is manufactured by a single manufacturer in a single production site.

subsections.title: Period of data collection
subsections.content: Manufacturer specific data have been collected for the year 2022.

subsections.title: Information on data collection
subsections.content:
Foreground processes include:
- Raw Materials used, their packaging, transportation mode and related quantities,
- Energy mix and amount used for production,
- Types of product packaging and related quantities,
- Environmental impacts of raw materials for which EPDs were available,
- All other quantitative information. 

Generic datasets were used for:
- Extraction and manufacturing of raw materials, packaging,
- Transportation and treatment of waste from raw material packaging and production,
- Waste treatment processes,
- Transport to end-of-life treatment,
- End-of-life treatment processes and scenarios.

– Declared value is representative of the representative product – Tilled Window System, which is also a worst case (highest impacts) of the product group covered by this EPD.

subsections.title: Database used for background data
subsections.content:
Databased used for modelling include:
- Ecoinvent 3.9
- Industry 2.0

subsections.title: Energy mix
subsections.content:
Aliplast purchases renewable energy mix from their supplier. Renewable energy mix was modelled based on the statistics for Belgium.
GWP of this mix is 0.049 kg CO2 eq. / kWh.
